content.problemStatement:
Chef has a tree with NNN nodes.

He has two extra nodes which he wants to be part of the tree.
Chef is curious about the diameter of the new tree.

Your task is to find the number of new trees with diameter **greater** than the older tree.

Note:


Both the new nodes can be possibly connected to same node in the tree.
Both the new nodes can be directly connected to each other.
For example, if the original tree is:



All these ways of adding new nodes aaa and bbb are fine as it results in a tree:






Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN, denoting the number of nodes in the existing tree.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges. The ithi^{th}ith of these N−1N-1N−1 lines contain two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output the number of new trees with diameter greater than the older tree.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^{6}106.
